---
title: "Compare Human, LLM, and Mixed Experiments"
output: html_document
---

This notebook is a lightweight starting point for comparing human-only,
LLM-only, and mixed experiments after all exports have been converted to the
shared schema.

```{r}
paths <- c(
  human_only = "../../sample_data/human_only/public_goods/player_round.csv",
  llm_only = "../../sample_data/llm_only/public_goods/player_round.csv",
  mixed = "../../sample_data/mixed_human_llm/public_goods/player_round.csv"
)

available <- paths[file.exists(paths)]

if (length(available) > 0) {
  datasets <- Map(function(path, mode) {
    data <- read.csv(path)
    data$mode <- mode
    data
  }, available, names(available))
  combined <- do.call(rbind, datasets)
  aggregate(action_value ~ mode + round + actor_type, data = combined, FUN = mean)
} else {
  message("No sample data found yet. Generate exports before knitting.")
}
```

Compare actor types and modes descriptively before fitting inferential models.
